commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Intelligent Gate: replace hardcoded deep-dive rules with AI-driven information density evaluation

- Remove DEEP_DIVE_HINTS (hardcoded Korean-language rules)
- Rewrite validateAndCheckDeepDive with language-agnostic 5W1H journalist
  evaluation prompt; returns `missing` field describing what's absent
- questionGuide and messageInstruction now driven by AI-generated `missing`
  instead of static category hints
- All deep-dive follow-up questions respond in the same language as the user
- Add `isDeepDive` to API request/response pipeline (frontend + backend)
- max_tokens increased 80→150 to accommodate `missing` field

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add LLM reranking for accurate source retrieval

Vector search returns 20 candidates, then Gemini 2.5 Flash scores
each chunk's TRUE topical relevance (0-10) to the user's question.
Only chunks scoring >= 3 are kept, sorted by relevance, top 8 used.

This fixes the core issue: vector search ranks chunks that merely
*mention* a topic equally with chunks *primarily about* that topic.
Reranking distinguishes the two — e.g., for "education system in
Uzbekistan", a post about academic preparedness (mentions education
in passing) now ranks below YouTube interviews directly discussing
education reform.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix 3 mobile UX issues: visible input bar, long-press select, swipe re-focus

Task 1 (#21): Input textarea is now a visible bar above quick-bar when keyboard
is open. Uses CSS class toggle (input-visible) so textarea stays focusable when
collapsed. Hides during scroll mode (_inScrollMode) and when keyboard closes.

Task 2 (#13): Long-press 500ms on terminal area triggers select mode with
auto-copy. Cancels if finger moves >5px (becomes scroll). selectionchange
listener auto-copies to clipboard. Select button remains as fallback.

Task 3: After swipe (touchMoved=true), re-focuses overlayInput if keyboard was
already open (_keyboardOpen flag), preventing input from breaking after scroll.

commit.message:
feat(ads): add TACoS metric to KPI cards and per-SKU table

TACoS (Total Advertising Cost of Sales) = Ad Spend / Total Gross Revenue
from all channels (organic + paid), vs ACoS which only uses ad-attributed
revenue. Summary TACoS from get_ledger_summary RPC; per-SKU TACoS from
marketplace_orders JSONB items. All 5 queries now run in parallel.

commit.message:
Fix: Convert all public pages to proper server/client component split for SEO

All public-facing pages were incorrectly marked "use client", which caused
generateMetadata to be silently ignored and Google Search Console redirect
errors across all pages.

Changes:
- products/[slug]: server page.tsx with generateMetadata + notFound(), new ProductClient.tsx
- categories/[slug]: server page.tsx with generateMetadata + notFound(), new CategoryClient.tsx
- blog/[slug]: full server component with generateMetadata, removes useEffect title hack
- contact: server wrapper with static metadata export, new ContactClient.tsx
- cart: server wrapper with static metadata export, new CartClient.tsx

Google now receives proper server-rendered HTML with correct titles, descriptions,
canonical URLs, and HTTP 404 for missing products/categories.

commit.message:
fix save state not saving model.safetensors with torch.compile

Manually save/load network weights in hooks to bypass accelerate's
default mechanism, which produces _orig_mod.-prefixed keys when
torch.compile wraps the model. Uses unwrap_model(keep_torch_compile=False)
before saving and pops models/weights to prevent double-saving.

commit.message:
fix: correct wallet symbol resolution and handle unpriced tokens

- resolveMint() was returning cgId.toUpperCase() (e.g. POCKET-NETWORK) instead
  of the actual token symbol (POKT) — causing $0 price for held tokens since
  market lookup used the wrong key. Now stores symbol in cache at resolve time.
- Added POKT and GRASS to CORE_TOKENS for instant resolution without cache
- buildLivePortfolio marks tokens with no price data as unpriced:true
- Claude context filters out unpriced tokens (SEEKER etc) — can't trade what can't be priced

commit.message:
Rewrite game logic: accept any legal move, check mate at the end

Instead of validating each move against the solution, the game now
accepts any legal move from the attacker. The defender responds with
the best defense (found via engine search). After all moves are
played, if the position is checkmate it's CLEAR, otherwise 不正解.

- Add findBestDefense() to shogi-core.js for optimal defender response
- Add puzzleResult state tracking ("clear" / "wrong" / null)
- Show 不正解 badge and "最初からやり直す" retry button
- Undo works from both wrong and in-progress states
- Update all e2e tests for new free-play mechanics (20 tests)

docs: v4 root cause corrected — insufficient steps not data poverty, v5 config + data pipeline (Refs #62, #63)

v4 used pretokenized-1024-v3 (5.3B unique tokens), not pretokenized-2048
(45M tokens). Plateau at val_ppl ~1000 was premature stopping (6% data
coverage at step 2,425), not data poverty. Corrected Five Whys in spec §8.3.

Added: v5 training config (40K steps, full epoch), pretokenize_parquet.py
script, CodeSearchNet Python data prep (133M tokens, merged to 180M),
ALB-082/083 gap register entries, scaling-law-prediction-v1.yaml in contract
registry.

Log version and build date at startup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add all 22 engineering improvements across hardware, firmware, ML, CV, and security

Hardware (1-6):
- Full potentiostat measurement architecture with control loop, TIA, feedback
- Auto-range current measurement (10 nA - 10 mA, 6 programmable TIA ranges)
- Noise control: 4-layer PCB, split analog/digital rails, shielding can, guard ring
- Temperature compensation via TMP117 (±0.1°C), Arrhenius-derived correction
- Gold pogo pin electrode contacts with mechanical guide slot (<5 Ohm variation)
- Dual calibration system: factory (flash) + field (standard solution), <5% error

Firmware (7-10):
- Deterministic TIM2-based waveform control (<10 us jitter)
- On-device signal conditioning pipeline (SG smoothing, ALS baseline, peak detection)
- Comprehensive fault detection (electrode, sample, contact, saturation, temp, cal)
- Power optimization: idle <2 uA, active <25 mA, full test ~0.7 mAh

App (11-15):
- Hardware abstraction layer (USB/BLE driver abstraction)
- Binary communication protocol with CRC-16 integrity
- Offline TFLite inference (<200 KB, <50 ms)
- Confidence scoring (model prob × SNR × quality → HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/RETEST)
- Autoencoder-based interference detection

ML (16-19):
- Physics-based synthetic voltammogram generator (Randles-Sevcik + augmentation)
- Multi-label CNN with detection + concentration dual heads
- Domain adaptation with metadata conditioning (TDS, pH, temp, water type)

CV (20-21):
- 10-patch color calibration system (grayscale + chromatic)
- Lighting correction (white balance, CLAHE, gamma correction)

Security (22-23):
- Ed25519 device authentication with signed data packets
- Server-side tamper detection (identical readings, impossible chemistry, outliers)

Updated BOM to $14.29 (honest, with part numbers).
Restructured project directories to match engineering spec.

commit.message:
Track E: decompressZstdWF skippable frame specs — skip branch + composition (#808)

* refactor: complete Zstd.lean quality pass — document grind + convert bare simp

Document the 2 remaining undocumented `grind` calls in
`decompressFrame_pos_gt` (both dictionary branches) following
the pattern established by PR #787. Convert 2 bare `simp` calls
in `decompressRawBlock_content` to `simp only` for robustness.

All 4 `grind` calls in Zstd.lean are now documented. All `simp`
calls use `only` form.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for Zstd.lean quality pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace bare simp with simp only in XxHash.lean

Convert 4 bare `simp` calls to `simp only` for proof stability.
Add inline comments to 3 `sorry` test vector theorems documenting
runtime verification location.

Bare simp: 4 → 0 (100% reduction)
Sorry count: 4 (unchanged, pre-existing UInt64 kernel timeout)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for XxHash.lean review

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Track E: BackwardBitReader.readBits totalBitsRemaining tracking — prove exact bit consumption (#803)

* feat: BackwardBitReader.readBits totalBitsRemaining tracking

Prove that readBits decreases totalBitsRemaining by exactly n bits:
- readBits_go_totalBitsRemaining: recursive helper decreases by k
- readBits_go_totalBitsRemaining_ge: success implies enough bits
- readBits_totalBitsRemaining_sub: public theorem, exact consumption
- readBits_totalBitsRemaining_lt: strict decrease for n > 0

These complete the readBits specification trifecta alongside
readBits_value_lt_pow2 (value bound) and the existing edge-case
theorems, enabling downstream proofs for Huffman decoding progress,
FSE state machine correctness, and sequence decoding bit budgets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for readBits totalBitsRemaining tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add concrete numeral ↓reduceIte case to lean-simp-tactics skill

↓reduceIte does not evaluate Decidable instances for concrete numeral
comparisons like (8 : Nat) = 0. Must provide explicit negation facts:
show ¬((8 : Nat) = 0) from by omega.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: decompressZstdWF skippable frame specs — skip branch + composition

Add two theorems completing three-branch coverage of decompressZstdWF:

- decompressZstdWF_single_skippable_frame: when a skippable frame is at
  pos and the position after skipping is past the end of data, the output
  is returned unchanged (skippable frames contribute no content).

- decompressZstdWF_skip_then_standard: when a skippable frame is followed
  by a standard Zstd frame, the result is output ++ content (only the
  standard frame contributes decompressed content).

Closes #801

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for skippable frame specs session
